rawText:
For decades the dollar reigned supreme as the world's reserve currency -- a status that allowed America to borrow cheaply, project financial power globally, and attract capital with an almost gravitational pull -- but a confluence of erratic trade policy, shrinking interest-rate differentials, and mounting investor anxiety has sent the greenback tumbling by roughly ten per cent since early 2025, raising the question of whether this decline marks a temporary correction or the beginning of a far more profound reckoning.

The erosion of faith in American assets, which once seemed unshakeable, stems in large part from the tariff mania that has defined President Trump's trade agenda, a patchwork of levies, exemptions, and threats so volatile that even the revenue they generate -- having plateaued at around $30 billion a month after surging through 2025 -- appears to have peaked, leaving businesses scrambling to reroute supply chains, obscure the origins of imports, or simply pass costs on to consumers who are already stretched thin.

What makes this episode of dollar weakness particularly treacherous is that the traditional safe-haven status of American Treasuries has begun to crack, with long-term yields rising rather than falling during recent market sell-offs -- a pattern more commonly associated with emerging-market crises than with the issuer of the world's reserve currency -- so that when investors panic, they now flee from American assets rather than towards them, a reversal that has occurred in seven of the past fifty-two weeks, roughly three times the historical frequency.

The structure of foreign investment in America has shifted in ways that amplify this vulnerability, as the share of foreign-owned American assets held in equities has nearly tripled since the global financial crisis to a record fifty-eight per cent, while sovereign debt holdings have shrunk to just thirteen per cent of total portfolio investment, meaning that the demand for dollars increasingly depends not on the cautious reserve management of central banks but on the far more fickle sentiment of profit-seeking investors who can -- and do -- change course swiftly.

Japan's predicament illustrates how the dollar's decline reverberates through even the most established economies, for the yen remains stubbornly weak despite the Bank of Japan's rate hikes from minus 0.1 per cent to 0.75 per cent over two years, weighed down by real yields of just 0.2 per cent that compare unfavourably with nearly two per cent in America, while the country's enormous public-sector balance sheet -- laden with foreign assets and resembling, as some economists argue, a leveraged hedge fund -- faces mark-to-market risks that could transform a slow-burning fiscal challenge into an outright currency crisis.

Tariff revenues, which the administration once touted as a pillar of fiscal strategy, are proving to be self-limiting in ways that further undermine the dollar's foundations: the 145 per cent levy imposed on Chinese imports at one point in April amounted to an effective embargo that generated no revenue at all, while Yale's Budget Lab estimates that fifteen per cent of tariff income is cancelled out by lower tax receipts elsewhere, and a looming Supreme Court decision -- with betting markets giving only a thirty per cent chance that the president's use of emergency powers will survive judicial scrutiny -- could dismantle much of the legal architecture on which these duties rest.

As hedging activity among foreign holders of American assets surges -- with pension funds such as Sweden's Alecta openly citing "reduced predictability" in American policy as the reason for trimming their Treasury holdings -- the mechanical process of selling dollars to cover unhedged exposure creates its own downward momentum, a self-reinforcing cycle in which a weaker greenback reduces the weight of American assets in global indices, forcing benchmark-tracking investors to sell further, which in turn pushes the currency lower still.

The nomination of Kevin Warsh to lead the Federal Reserve, though initially calming to markets because of his hawkish credentials, introduces fresh uncertainty, for his recent conversion to dovish rhetoric -- advocating rate cuts on the grounds that artificial intelligence will deliver a productivity miracle capable of suppressing inflation -- sits uneasily with an economy in which inflation remains above target at 2.8 per cent and fiscal stimulus from tax cuts and potential tariff refunds could push prices higher, making the case for monetary easing look increasingly ill-timed.

A historical parallel looms large: between 2002 and 2008, following the dotcom bust, American stocks underperformed European and emerging-market bourses while the dollar lost roughly forty per cent of its value, and yet that decline occurred during a period when central banks worldwide were still accumulating dollar reserves at a healthy clip -- a backstop that may not materialise this time, given that the dollar's share of global foreign-exchange reserves has already dropped from seventy-two per cent in 1999 to fifty-seven per cent today, with gold hoarding and diversification into alternative currencies accelerating.

No credible rival has emerged to supplant the dollar as the anchor of global finance, and the sheer volume, liquidity, and legal certainty of Treasury markets remain unmatched -- yet as one Fed official warned in 2010, the reserve-currency privilege "is not our birthright" but must be "earned and re-earned," a maxim that grows more urgent with each spasm of policy-driven volatility, each erosion of institutional credibility, and each wave of capital that quietly begins the long journey elsewhere.

overview.title: The Falling Dollar and What It Means for the World

sentences.original: For decades the dollar reigned supreme as the world's reserve currency -- a status that allowed America to borrow cheaply, project financial power globally, and attract capital with an almost gravitational pull -- but a confluence of erratic trade policy, shrinking interest-rate differentials, and mounting investor anxiety has sent the greenback tumbling by roughly ten per cent since early 2025, raising the question of whether this decline marks a temporary correction or the beginning of a far more profound reckoning.
sentences.translation: 수십 년간 달러는 세계 기축통화로 군림했다 — 미국이 저렴하게 차입하고, 전 세계에 금융 권력을 투사하며, 거의 중력과 같은 힘으로 자본을 끌어당길 수 있게 해준 지위였다 — 그러나 변덕스러운 무역 정책, 축소되는 금리 격차, 그리고 커져가는 투자자 불안이 합류하면서 2025년 초 이후 달러화 가치가 약 10% 하락했고, 이 하락이 일시적 조정인지 훨씬 더 심대한 청산의 시작인지에 대한 의문을 제기하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: For decades the dollar reigned supreme
grammarElements.label: 시간부사구 + 주절

grammarElements.text: a status that allowed America to borrow cheaply, project financial power globally, and attract capital
grammarElements.label: 대시 안 동격 명사구 + to부정사 3개 병렬

grammarElements.text: but a confluence of ... has sent the greenback tumbling
grammarElements.label: 전환절 (send + O + V-ing)

grammarElements.text: raising the question of whether ... or ...
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 + whether A or B 간접의문

vocabulary.example: A confluence of factors drove the market downturn.

vocabulary.meaning: 미국 달러의 별칭 (지폐 뒷면이 녹색인 데서 유래)
vocabulary.example: The greenback weakened sharply against major currencies.

expressions.usage: 장기간에 걸쳐 압도적 우위를 유지했음을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: For decades [subject] reigned supreme as [status], but [change] has sent [it] tumbling
patterns.explanation: 오랜 우위가 갑작스러운 변화에 의해 흔들리는 서사 구조
examples: For decades the yen reigned supreme as Asia's safe haven, but capital outflows have sent it tumbling.
For decades the pound reigned supreme in global trade, but two wars sent it tumbling.

drillQuestions.question: A confluence of erratic trade policy and mounting investor anxiety has sent the greenback ______ by roughly ten per cent.

sentences.notes: 기사의 도입부로서, 달러의 과거 우위(reigned supreme)와 현재 하락(tumbling)을 but으로 극적으로 대조한다. greenback은 경제 기사에서 dollar 대신 자주 쓰이는 별칭이다.

koreanAlignment.en: For decades the dollar reigned supreme
a confluence of erratic trade policy
has sent the greenback tumbling
a temporary correction or the beginning of a far more profound reckoning
koreanAlignment.ko: 일시적 조정인지 훨씬 더 심대한 청산의 시작인지

sentences.original: The erosion of faith in American assets, which once seemed unshakeable, stems in large part from the tariff mania that has defined President Trump's trade agenda, a patchwork of levies, exemptions, and threats so volatile that even the revenue they generate -- having plateaued at around $30 billion a month after surging through 2025 -- appears to have peaked, leaving businesses scrambling to reroute supply chains, obscure the origins of imports, or simply pass costs on to consumers who are already stretched thin.
sentences.translation: 한때 흔들림 없어 보였던 미국 자산에 대한 신뢰의 침식은 상당 부분 트럼프 대통령의 무역 의제를 규정해온 관세 광풍에서 비롯된다. 부과금, 면제, 위협이 뒤섞인 누더기 정책은 너무나 변덕스러워서 그것이 만들어내는 세수마저도 — 2025년을 거치며 급증한 뒤 월 약 300억 달러에서 정체된 — 정점을 찍은 것으로 보이며, 기업들은 공급망을 우회하거나, 수입품의 원산지를 감추거나, 이미 빠듯한 소비자에게 비용을 떠넘기느라 허둥대고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The erosion of faith in American assets
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: which once seemed unshakeable
grammarElements.label: 삽입 관계절

grammarElements.text: stems in large part from the tariff mania
grammarElements.label: 동사구 (stem from)

grammarElements.text: leaving businesses scrambling to reroute ..., obscure ..., or simply pass costs on ...
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (leave + O + V-ing + to부정사 3개 병렬)

vocabulary.meaning: 누더기, 조각보 (여기서는 체계 없이 짜깁기된 정책을 비유)
vocabulary.example: The regulations amount to a patchwork of contradictory rules.

expressions.usage: 자원이나 사람이 감당 가능한 한계에 근접한 상태를 표현

patterns.pattern: The erosion of [trust/faith] in [institution] stems in large part from [cause]
patterns.explanation: 신뢰 침식의 주요 원인을 제시하는 패턴
examples: The erosion of trust in the banking system stems in large part from repeated scandals.
The erosion of faith in public institutions stems in large part from years of broken promises.

drillQuestions.question: The erosion of faith in American assets stems in large part ______ the tariff mania.

sentences.notes: patchwork라는 비유가 관세 정책의 비체계적 성격을 효과적으로 전달한다. stretched thin은 일상적으로도 자주 쓰이는 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The erosion of faith in American assets
stems in large part from the tariff mania
a patchwork of levies, exemptions, and threats
koreanAlignment.ko: 부과금, 면제, 위협이 뒤섞인 누더기 정책

koreanAlignment.en: consumers who are already stretched thin

sentences.original: What makes this episode of dollar weakness particularly treacherous is that the traditional safe-haven status of American Treasuries has begun to crack, with long-term yields rising rather than falling during recent market sell-offs -- a pattern more commonly associated with emerging-market crises than with the issuer of the world's reserve currency -- so that when investors panic, they now flee from American assets rather than towards them, a reversal that has occurred in seven of the past fifty-two weeks, roughly three times the historical frequency.
sentences.translation: 이번 달러 약세가 특히 위험한 것은 미국 국채의 전통적 안전자산 지위에 금이 가기 시작했다는 점이다. 최근 시장 매도세 때 장기 금리가 하락하지 않고 오히려 상승했는데, 이는 기축통화 발행국보다는 신흥시장 위기에서 흔히 보이는 패턴이다. 그래서 투자자들이 패닉에 빠지면 이제 미국 자산으로 도피하는 것이 아니라 미국 자산에서 이탈한다 — 이런 역전은 지난 52주 중 7주에서 발생했으며, 역사적 빈도의 대략 3배에 달한다.

grammarElements.text: What makes this episode of dollar weakness particularly treacherous
grammarElements.label: What절 주어

grammarElements.text: is that the traditional safe-haven status ... has begun to crack
grammarElements.label: be동사 + that절 보어

grammarElements.text: with long-term yields rising rather than falling
grammarElements.label: with + O + V-ing 부대상황

grammarElements.text: a reversal that has occurred in seven of the past fifty-two weeks
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.meaning: 위험한, 기만적인 (겉보기와 달리 위험이 숨어 있는)
vocabulary.example: The calm market conditions proved treacherous for short sellers.

expressions.usage: 오랫동안 견고해 보였던 것이 약해지기 시작할 때 사용하는 비유

patterns.pattern: What makes [situation] particularly [adjective] is that [unexpected development]
patterns.explanation: 특정 상황이 왜 유독 심각한지 그 핵심을 제시하는 패턴
examples: What makes this recession particularly painful is that it follows a decade of stagnation.
What makes this deal particularly risky is that regulators may block it.

drillQuestions.question: What makes this episode of dollar weakness particularly treacherous is that the traditional safe-haven status has begun to ______.

sentences.notes: 안전자산에서 위험자산으로의 인식 전환이라는 전례 없는 상황을 묘사한다. rather than이 두 번 쓰여 기대와 현실의 괴리를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What makes this episode particularly treacherous
koreanAlignment.ko: 이번 달러 약세가 특히 위험한 것은

koreanAlignment.en: the traditional safe-haven status has begun to crack
koreanAlignment.ko: 전통적 안전자산 지위에 금이 가기 시작했다

koreanAlignment.en: they now flee from American assets rather than towards them
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국 자산으로 도피하는 것이 아니라 미국 자산에서 이탈한다

koreanAlignment.en: roughly three times the historical frequency

sentences.original: The structure of foreign investment in America has shifted in ways that amplify this vulnerability, as the share of foreign-owned American assets held in equities has nearly tripled since the global financial crisis to a record fifty-eight per cent, while sovereign debt holdings have shrunk to just thirteen per cent of total portfolio investment, meaning that the demand for dollars increasingly depends not on the cautious reserve management of central banks but on the far more fickle sentiment of profit-seeking investors who can -- and do -- change course swiftly.
sentences.translation: 미국에 대한 외국인 투자 구조가 이 취약성을 증폭하는 방향으로 변화했다. 외국인이 보유한 미국 자산 중 주식 비중은 글로벌 금융위기 이후 거의 3배로 늘어 사상 최고인 58%에 달한 반면, 국채 보유 비중은 전체 포트폴리오 투자의 13%로 줄었다. 이는 달러 수요가 점점 중앙은행의 신중한 외환보유고 관리가 아니라, 방향을 빠르게 — 실제로도 — 바꿀 수 있는 이익 추구 투자자들의 훨씬 더 변덕스러운 심리에 의존하게 되었음을 뜻한다.

grammarElements.text: The structure of foreign investment in America
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has shifted in ways that amplify this vulnerability
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: as the share ... has nearly tripled ... while sovereign debt holdings have shrunk
grammarElements.label: as절 내 대조 (tripled vs. shrunk)

grammarElements.text: not on ... but on ...
grammarElements.label: not A but B 대조 구조

vocabulary.example: Consumer tastes are notoriously fickle in the fashion industry.

expressions.usage: 투자자나 정책 입안자가 전략을 갑자기 바꿀 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Demand] depends not on [stable factor] but on [volatile factor]
patterns.explanation: 수요나 체계의 기반이 안정적인 것에서 불안정한 것으로 옮겨갔음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Growth depends not on domestic savings but on volatile capital inflows.
Stability depends not on institutional strength but on market confidence.

drillQuestions.question: The demand for dollars increasingly depends not on central banks but on the far more ______ sentiment of profit-seeking investors.

sentences.notes: who can -- and do -- change course swiftly에서 대시 사이의 and do가 이론적 가능성이 아니라 실제 현실임을 강조하는 수사적 장치다.

koreanAlignment.en: The structure of foreign investment in America has shifted
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국에 대한 외국인 투자 구조가 변화했다

koreanAlignment.en: has nearly tripled since the global financial crisis
the far more fickle sentiment of profit-seeking investors
koreanAlignment.ko: 이익 추구 투자자들의 훨씬 더 변덕스러운 심리

koreanAlignment.en: who can -- and do -- change course swiftly
koreanAlignment.ko: 방향을 빠르게 바꿀 수 있는 — 실제로도 바꾸는

pronunciationNotes: fickle은 /ˈfɪkəl/ — 짧고 날카롭게 발음, 'pickle'과 비슷한 운율
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: parenthetical emphasis (삽입구를 통한 강조: and do)

sentences.original: Japan's predicament illustrates how the dollar's decline reverberates through even the most established economies, for the yen remains stubbornly weak despite the Bank of Japan's rate hikes from minus 0.1 per cent to 0.75 per cent over two years, weighed down by real yields of just 0.2 per cent that compare unfavourably with nearly two per cent in America, while the country's enormous public-sector balance sheet -- laden with foreign assets and resembling, as some economists argue, a leveraged hedge fund -- faces mark-to-market risks that could transform a slow-burning fiscal challenge into an outright currency crisis.
sentences.translation: 일본의 곤경은 달러 하락이 가장 확고한 경제에까지 어떻게 파급되는지를 보여준다. 엔화는 일본은행이 2년간 금리를 -0.1%에서 0.75%로 인상했음에도 여전히 완고하게 약세를 보이고 있는데, 이는 미국의 약 2%에 비해 불리한 실질수익률 0.2%에 눌려 있기 때문이다. 한편 일부 경제학자들이 레버리지 헤지펀드에 비유하는, 외국 자산으로 가득 찬 국가의 거대한 공공부문 대차대조표는 서서히 타오르던 재정 과제를 본격적인 통화위기로 바꿀 수 있는 시가평가 위험에 직면해 있다.

grammarElements.text: Japan's predicament
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: illustrates how the dollar's decline reverberates through ...
grammarElements.label: 동사 + how절 목적어

grammarElements.text: for the yen remains stubbornly weak despite ...
grammarElements.label: 이유를 나타내는 for절 + despite 양보

grammarElements.text: laden with foreign assets and resembling ... a leveraged hedge fund
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 + 현재분사 삽입구

vocabulary.example: The central bank found itself in a predicament with no easy exit.

expressions.usage: 즉각적이지는 않지만 점진적으로 심각해지는 문제를 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Country]'s predicament illustrates how [global trend] reverberates through [affected area]
patterns.explanation: 특정 국가의 사례가 글로벌 추세의 파급효과를 보여주는 구조
examples: Turkey's predicament illustrates how inflation reverberates through emerging economies.
Germany's predicament illustrates how energy costs reverberate through manufacturing sectors.

drillQuestions.question: Japan's predicament illustrates how the dollar's decline ______ through even the most established economies.

sentences.notes: 일본의 공공부문 대차대조표를 'leveraged hedge fund'에 비유하는 것은 매우 도발적인 표현이다. mark-to-market은 자산을 시장 가격으로 재평가하는 것을 뜻하는 금융 용어다.

koreanAlignment.en: Japan's predicament
the yen remains stubbornly weak
resembling a leveraged hedge fund
a slow-burning fiscal challenge into an outright currency crisis
koreanAlignment.ko: 서서히 타오르던 재정 과제를 본격적인 통화위기로

sentences.original: Tariff revenues, which the administration once touted as a pillar of fiscal strategy, are proving to be self-limiting in ways that further undermine the dollar's foundations: the 145 per cent levy imposed on Chinese imports at one point in April amounted to an effective embargo that generated no revenue at all, while Yale's Budget Lab estimates that fifteen per cent of tariff income is cancelled out by lower tax receipts elsewhere, and a looming Supreme Court decision -- with betting markets giving only a thirty per cent chance that the president's use of emergency powers will survive judicial scrutiny -- could dismantle much of the legal architecture on which these duties rest.
sentences.translation: 행정부가 한때 재정 전략의 기둥으로 내세웠던 관세 수입은 달러의 기반을 더욱 약화시키는 방식으로 자기 제한적임이 드러나고 있다. 4월 한때 중국산 수입품에 부과된 145% 관세는 사실상 수입을 차단하는 금수 조치여서 세수를 전혀 만들지 못했고, 예일대 예산연구소는 관세 수입의 15%가 다른 곳에서의 세수 감소로 상쇄된다고 추산하며, 다가오는 대법원 판결 — 베팅 시장은 대통령의 비상권한 사용이 사법 심사를 통과할 확률을 30%로 보고 있다 — 은 이들 관세가 기반하고 있는 법적 구조 상당 부분을 해체할 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: Tariff revenues, which the administration once touted as a pillar of fiscal strategy
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 삽입 관계절

grammarElements.text: are proving to be self-limiting
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사 (prove to be)

grammarElements.text: the 145 per cent levy ... amounted to an effective embargo
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 첫 번째 예시절 (amount to)

grammarElements.text: a looming Supreme Court decision ... could dismantle much of the legal architecture
grammarElements.label: 세 번째 예시절

vocabulary.example: Extremely high tax rates can be self-limiting as people find ways to avoid them.

expressions.usage: 공식적으로는 아니지만 실질적 효과가 동일한 상황을 표현

patterns.pattern: [Policy], which [actor] once touted as [positive claim], is proving to be [negative reality]
patterns.explanation: 정책이 내세웠던 장점과 달리 부작용을 드러내는 구조
examples: The reform, which officials once touted as a breakthrough, is proving to be unworkable.
The tax cut, which the party once touted as growth-boosting, is proving to be fiscally reckless.

drillQuestions.question: Tariff revenues are proving to be ______ in ways that further undermine the dollar's foundations.

sentences.notes: 콜론 뒤에 세 가지 구체적 근거를 나열하며 관세 정책의 자기 모순을 보여준다. amount to는 '결과적으로 ~에 해당하다'라는 뜻으로 자주 쓰인다.

koreanAlignment.en: which the administration once touted as a pillar of fiscal strategy
koreanAlignment.ko: 행정부가 한때 재정 전략의 기둥으로 내세웠던

koreanAlignment.en: are proving to be self-limiting
amounted to an effective embargo
could dismantle much of the legal architecture
koreanAlignment.ko: 법적 구조 상당 부분을 해체할 수 있다

sentences.original: As hedging activity among foreign holders of American assets surges -- with pension funds such as Sweden's Alecta openly citing "reduced predictability" in American policy as the reason for trimming their Treasury holdings -- the mechanical process of selling dollars to cover unhedged exposure creates its own downward momentum, a self-reinforcing cycle in which a weaker greenback reduces the weight of American assets in global indices, forcing benchmark-tracking investors to sell further, which in turn pushes the currency lower still.
sentences.translation: 미국 자산 외국인 보유자들 사이에서 헤징 활동이 급증하면서 — 스웨덴의 알렉타 같은 연기금이 미국 정책의 "예측 가능성 저하"를 국채 보유 축소의 이유로 공개적으로 언급하고 있다 — 미헤지 노출을 메우기 위해 달러를 매도하는 기계적 과정이 자체적으로 하락 모멘텀을 만들어내고 있다. 이는 약한 달러가 글로벌 지수에서 미국 자산의 비중을 줄이고, 벤치마크 추종 투자자들이 추가 매도를 하도록 강제하며, 이것이 다시 통화를 더 끌어내리는 자기 강화적 순환이다.

grammarElements.text: As hedging activity ... surges
grammarElements.label: 시간/상황 부사절 (As)

grammarElements.text: with pension funds ... openly citing ... as the reason
grammarElements.label: with + O + V-ing 부대상황

grammarElements.text: the mechanical process of selling dollars ... creates its own downward momentum
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: a self-reinforcing cycle in which ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.meaning: 헤징, 위험 회피 (환율 변동 위험을 줄이기 위한 금융 거래)
vocabulary.example: The cost of hedging against dollar weakness has risen sharply.

expressions.usage: 원인과 결과가 서로를 증폭시키는 악순환 또는 선순환을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Process] creates its own downward momentum, a self-reinforcing cycle in which [step 1], forcing [step 2], which in turn [step 3]
patterns.explanation: 연쇄적 인과관계가 자기 강화적 순환을 만드는 구조
examples: Capital flight creates its own downward momentum, a self-reinforcing cycle in which falling prices scare investors, forcing fire sales, which in turn depress prices further.
Brain drain creates its own downward momentum, a self-reinforcing cycle in which talent leaves, forcing firms to relocate, which in turn pushes more workers abroad.

drillQuestions.question: The mechanical process of selling dollars creates its own downward momentum, a ______-reinforcing cycle.

sentences.notes: 금융 시장의 feedback loop(피드백 고리)를 단계별로 보여주는 문장이다. 벤치마크 추종 투자의 기계적 매도가 통화 약세를 더 깊게 만드는 메커니즘을 이해하는 것이 핵심이다.

koreanAlignment.en: hedging activity among foreign holders surges
reduced predictability in American policy
creates its own downward momentum
a self-reinforcing cycle

pronunciationNotes: hedging은 /ˈhedʒɪŋ/ — hedge의 동명사, '헤징'으로 한국어에서도 그대로 사용

sentences.original: The nomination of Kevin Warsh to lead the Federal Reserve, though initially calming to markets because of his hawkish credentials, introduces fresh uncertainty, for his recent conversion to dovish rhetoric -- advocating rate cuts on the grounds that artificial intelligence will deliver a productivity miracle capable of suppressing inflation -- sits uneasily with an economy in which inflation remains above target at 2.8 per cent and fiscal stimulus from tax cuts and potential tariff refunds could push prices higher, making the case for monetary easing look increasingly ill-timed.
sentences.translation: 케빈 워시의 연방준비제도 의장 지명은 그의 매파적 경력 덕분에 처음에는 시장을 안정시켰으나 새로운 불확실성을 도입하고 있다. 인공지능이 인플레이션을 억제할 생산성 기적을 가져올 것이라는 근거로 금리 인하를 주장하는 그의 최근 비둘기파 전환이, 인플레이션이 여전히 목표치를 상회하는 2.8%이고 감세와 잠재적 관세 환급에 의한 재정 부양이 물가를 더 밀어올릴 수 있는 경제 환경과 맞지 않기 때문이며, 이는 통화 완화의 논거를 점점 더 시기적절하지 못한 것으로 보이게 한다.

grammarElements.text: The nomination of Kevin Warsh to lead the Federal Reserve
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: though initially calming to markets because of his hawkish credentials
grammarElements.label: 양보 분사구

grammarElements.text: introduces fresh uncertainty
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사

grammarElements.text: for his recent conversion to dovish rhetoric ... sits uneasily with ...
grammarElements.label: 이유를 나타내는 for절

vocabulary.example: The central bank struck a hawkish tone, signalling further rate increases.
Markets rallied on the dovish pivot from the Fed chair.

expressions.usage: 두 가지 요소가 논리적·상황적으로 충돌할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Appointment], though initially [positive reaction], introduces fresh uncertainty, for [contradictory detail]
patterns.explanation: 인사가 처음에는 긍정적이었으나 모순점이 불확실성을 야기하는 구조
examples: The new CEO, though initially welcomed by investors, introduces fresh uncertainty, for his track record includes two failed turnarounds.
The trade deal, though initially calming to markets, introduces fresh uncertainty, for key enforcement provisions remain vague.

drillQuestions.question: His recent conversion to dovish rhetoric sits ______ with an economy in which inflation remains above target.

sentences.notes: hawkish와 dovish는 금융 기사의 필수 어휘다. 매(hawk)가 공격적 긴축을, 비둘기(dove)가 온건한 완화를 상징한다. 워시의 갑작스러운 전환이 시장 혼란을 키우는 아이러니를 포착하고 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: The nomination of Kevin Warsh to lead the Federal Reserve
his hawkish credentials
his recent conversion to dovish rhetoric
making the case for monetary easing look increasingly ill-timed
koreanAlignment.ko: 통화 완화의 논거를 점점 더 시기적절하지 못한 것으로 보이게 한다

pronunciationNotes: hawkish는 /ˈhɔːkɪʃ/, dovish는 /ˈdʌvɪʃ/ — 둘 다 동물 이름 + ish 접미사

sentences.original: A historical parallel looms large: between 2002 and 2008, following the dotcom bust, American stocks underperformed European and emerging-market bourses while the dollar lost roughly forty per cent of its value, and yet that decline occurred during a period when central banks worldwide were still accumulating dollar reserves at a healthy clip -- a backstop that may not materialise this time, given that the dollar's share of global foreign-exchange reserves has already dropped from seventy-two per cent in 1999 to fifty-seven per cent today, with gold hoarding and diversification into alternative currencies accelerating.
sentences.translation: 역사적 유사점이 크게 부각된다. 2002년에서 2008년 사이, 닷컴 버블 붕괴 이후 미국 주식은 유럽 및 신흥시장 증시에 뒤처졌고 달러는 가치의 약 40%를 잃었다. 그러나 그 하락은 전 세계 중앙은행이 여전히 건실한 속도로 달러 보유고를 축적하던 시기에 일어났다 — 이번에는 그 안전장치가 실현되지 않을 수 있는데, 달러의 글로벌 외환보유고 비중이 이미 1999년 72%에서 오늘날 57%로 떨어졌고, 금 비축과 대체 통화로의 다각화가 가속하고 있기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: A historical parallel looms large
grammarElements.label: 주절 (loom large = 크게 부각되다)

grammarElements.text: between 2002 and 2008 ... American stocks underperformed ... while the dollar lost ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 역사적 서술절

grammarElements.text: and yet that decline occurred during a period when ...
grammarElements.label: 역접 (and yet) + 관계부사절

grammarElements.text: a backstop that may not materialise this time, given that ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + given that 조건절

vocabulary.example: Central bank reserves served as a backstop during previous dollar declines.

expressions.usage: 중요한 문제나 위험이 점점 가까이 다가올 때 사용

patterns.pattern: A historical parallel looms large: [past event], and yet [key difference this time]
patterns.explanation: 역사적 비교를 제시한 뒤 이번에는 상황이 다름을 강조하는 패턴
examples: A historical parallel looms large: Japan's lost decade began with asset bubbles, and yet today's debt levels are far higher.
A historical parallel looms large: the 1970s stagflation followed oil shocks, and yet the current supply disruptions are more widespread.

drillQuestions.question: A historical parallel ______ large: between 2002 and 2008, the dollar lost roughly forty per cent of its value.

sentences.notes: 과거 사례와의 비교를 통해 현재 상황이 더 위험할 수 있음을 보여주는 전형적 분석 기사 구조다. backstop은 야구에서 유래한 비유로, 금융에서 '최후의 안전장치'를 뜻한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A historical parallel looms large
American stocks underperformed European and emerging-market bourses
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국 주식은 유럽 및 신흥시장 증시에 뒤처졌고

koreanAlignment.en: a backstop that may not materialise this time
koreanAlignment.ko: 이번에는 실현되지 않을 수 있는 안전장치

koreanAlignment.en: gold hoarding and diversification into alternative currencies accelerating
koreanAlignment.ko: 금 비축과 대체 통화로의 다각화가 가속하고 있다

sentences.original: No credible rival has emerged to supplant the dollar as the anchor of global finance, and the sheer volume, liquidity, and legal certainty of Treasury markets remain unmatched -- yet as one Fed official warned in 2010, the reserve-currency privilege "is not our birthright" but must be "earned and re-earned," a maxim that grows more urgent with each spasm of policy-driven volatility, each erosion of institutional credibility, and each wave of capital that quietly begins the long journey elsewhere.
sentences.translation: 달러를 글로벌 금융의 닻으로서 대체할 믿을 만한 경쟁자는 아직 등장하지 않았고, 국채 시장의 순수한 규모, 유동성, 법적 확실성은 여전히 타의 추종을 불허한다 — 그러나 한 연준 관리가 2010년에 경고했듯이, 기축통화 특권은 "태어나면서부터 주어진 권리가 아니라" "벌고 또 벌어야 하는 것"이며, 이 격언은 정책이 촉발한 변동성의 경련이 있을 때마다, 제도적 신뢰가 침식될 때마다, 그리고 자본이 조용히 다른 곳으로의 먼 여정을 시작하는 물결이 있을 때마다 더 절박해지고 있다.

grammarElements.text: No credible rival has emerged to supplant the dollar
grammarElements.label: 부정 주어 + 현재완료

grammarElements.text: the sheer volume, liquidity, and legal certainty ... remain unmatched
grammarElements.label: 병렬 주어 + remain + 보어

grammarElements.text: yet as one Fed official warned
grammarElements.label: 역접 + 인용 도입절

grammarElements.text: a maxim that grows more urgent with each ... each ... and each ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + each 3회 반복 (삼중 병렬)

vocabulary.example: Digital payments have yet to supplant cash in most developing nations.
Access to clean water should be every citizen's birthright.

expressions.usage: 특권이나 지위가 자동으로 유지되는 것이 아니라 지속적 노력이 필요함을 강조

patterns.pattern: [Privilege] is not [guaranteed status] but must be [actively maintained], a maxim that grows more urgent with each [recurring problem]
patterns.explanation: 특권이 자동으로 주어지는 것이 아니라 유지 노력이 필요하다는 결론 패턴
examples: Democracy is not a guaranteed state but must be defended, a maxim that grows more urgent with each assault on the rule of law.
Market leadership is not a permanent position but must be earned, a maxim that grows more urgent with each wave of disruption.

drillQuestions.question: No credible rival has emerged to ______ the dollar as the anchor of global finance.

sentences.notes: 기사의 결론부로서, 달러의 현재 우위를 인정하면서도 '벌고 또 벌어야 하는 특권'이라는 인용으로 미래에 대한 경고를 남긴다. each의 세 번 반복(anaphora)이 위기 요인을 리듬감 있게 나열한다.

koreanAlignment.en: No credible rival has emerged to supplant the dollar
koreanAlignment.ko: 달러를 대체할 믿을 만한 경쟁자는 아직 등장하지 않았다

koreanAlignment.en: is not our birthright
must be earned and re-earned
each wave of capital that quietly begins the long journey elsewhere
koreanAlignment.ko: 자본이 조용히 다른 곳으로의 먼 여정을 시작하는 물결

pronunciationNotes: supplant는 /səˈplænt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세, supplement와 혼동 주의
